items.q: How did Hume define a miracle?
items.correct: A violation of the laws of nature by a particular volition of the Deity — and he argued evidence for such violations could never outweigh the uniform experience supporting the laws.
items.misconception: Hume defined a miracle as something amazing or unexpected.
items.random_wrong: Hume believed all miracles in the Bible really happened.
items.why: Hume's definition is technical — a violation of natural law. Students soften it to mean 'remarkable event'. The strict definition is what makes his argument against believing in miracles work.

items.q: How do Muslims believe the Quran was revealed?
items.correct: Through the angel Jibril (Gabriel) to Muhammad over 23 years; Muslims believe it is the literal word of God, not Muhammad's own words.
items.misconception: Muhammad wrote the Quran based on his own teachings.
items.random_wrong: The Quran was written by Allah and dropped from heaven.
items.why: Authorship is theologically critical in Islam — the Quran is held to be God's exact speech transmitted via Gabriel. Students treat Muhammad as author, which contradicts core Islamic belief.

items.q: What do Christians mean by resurrection, and how does it differ from reincarnation?
items.correct: Resurrection is the once-for-all bodily raising to eternal life at the end of time (modelled on Christ's resurrection); reincarnation is the soul's repeated rebirth into new bodies. They are distinct doctrines from different religious traditions.
items.misconception: Resurrection and reincarnation are basically the same idea of life after death.
items.random_wrong: Christians believe everyone is reincarnated until they accept Jesus.
items.why: Christianity affirms one bodily resurrection, not cyclical rebirth. Students flatten different afterlife concepts into a single 'life after death' idea, missing the very different metaphysics involved.
Correct answer: Gustavo Gutierrez. “Leonardo Boff” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Which dialogue contains the divine-command dilemma?
items.why: Correct answer: Euthyphro. “Republic” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many precepts are in basic Buddhist lay ethics?
items.why: The real figure is 5 precepts. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
